title: The Growing Threat of Cybercrime
description: For questions 17 – 24, read the text below. Use the word given in capitals at the end of some of the lines to form a word that fits in the gap in the same line. There is an example at the beginning (0).

content.text: Cybercrime is one of the fastest-growing (0) criminal activities in the modern world, posing a serious threat to individuals, businesses, and governments alike. Hackers and online fraudsters are becoming increasingly (17) sophisticated, using advanced techniques to steal data and money. Financial institutions spend billions every year on (18) protection against cyber attacks, yet serious breaches still occur. Accessing someone's private accounts without permission is entirely (19) illegal and can result in severe penalties. A key part of reducing cybercrime is building greater (20) awareness among ordinary internet users about how to stay safe online. Acting in an (21) irresponsible way, such as using weak passwords or clicking unknown links, creates opportunities for criminals. Governments and technology companies are working together on strategies for the (22) prevention of large-scale cyber attacks. The financial (23) damage caused by cybercrime continues to grow year on year. Building systems and networks that are fully (24) reliable requires both technical expertise and constant vigilance.

example.text: Cybercrime is one of the fastest-growing (0) CRIMINAL activities in the modern world. (CRIME)

questions.explanation: An adjective is needed after 'increasingly'; 'sophisticate' becomes 'sophisticated' by adding the suffix '-d', meaning 'highly advanced'.
A noun is needed after 'on'; 'protect' becomes 'protection' by adding the suffix '-ion'.
A negative adjective is needed; the prefix 'il-' is added to 'legal' to mean 'against the law'.
A noun is needed after 'greater'; 'aware' becomes 'awareness' by adding the suffix '-ness'.
A negative adjective is needed; the prefix 'ir-' is added to 'responsible' to mean 'not acting carefully'.
A noun is needed after 'the'; 'prevent' becomes 'prevention' by adding the suffix '-ion'.
An adjective is needed before 'damage'; 'signify' becomes 'significant' by changing '-ify' to '-icant'.
An adjective is needed after 'fully'; 'rely' becomes 'reliable' by changing '-y' to '-iable'.
